definition: Each central bank optimizes QT pacing domestically while generating cross-border externalities through capital flows, exchange rates, and term premia spillovers. The game-theoretic structure of simultaneous multi-central-bank tightening creates coordination challenges that are underexplored analytically.
evidence: G3 central banks simultaneously normalizing balance sheets
Cross-border capital flow effects of BoJ normalization on UST demand
open_questions: Is there implicit coordination among G3 central banks on QT pacing?
Which central bank's normalization creates the largest cross-border spillovers?
